wrongClaims.ourClaim: Concept of Freedom presents pianist Duke Ellington working in a swing vein
wrongClaims.problem: MB identifies the primary credited artist of this 2005 hatOLOGY release as Anthony Braxton (Duke Ellington is co-credited because the work overlays Ellington's Second Sacred Concert 'Freedom' material); framing it as a Duke Ellington swing-vein leader date misattributes the album. Duke Ellington died in 1974 and was not a 2005 recording leader.

notes: The release itself is correctly identified (title 'Concept of Freedom', label hatOLOGY, year 2005, and all 5 keyTracks -- the long 'Comp. No. 257 ... / Freedom No. 1, 4 & 6 From the Sacred Concert No. 2' titles -- match MB exactly), so this is factual_error rather than entity_mismatch. The error is the artist attribution/characterization: MB lists Anthony Braxton FIRST. The track titles use Braxton's composition-numbering system ('Comp. No. 257'); hatOLOGY is a Braxton label home. This is a 2005 avant-garde Braxton work, not a Duke Ellington swing/big-band date. The 'swing vein'/'big band' genre language is editorially uncheckable on its own (no genre field in evidence) but the leader attribution is the concrete, evidence-backed error. No wikipedia evidence (opensearch returned nothing).

MB embedded_mbid confirms title(Parish)/year(2005)/label(ECM Records; ours 'ECM' is the same label). Wiki stored_url is the Bobo Stenson artist bio (right entity), and the DNA's Bobo Stenson Trio sentence (Anders Jormin, Jon Falt, 'over five decades') is verbatim from it. CAVEAT for repair: MB credits four co-equal artists with Thomas Stronen listed FIRST (Stronen/Stenson/Ljungkvist/Eilertsen), and the Stenson wiki files Parish under sideman/'With Thomas Stronen' -- so 'pianist Bobo Stenson's 2005 album' may overstate his leadership. No clear single-leader field is contradicted and Stenson is a genuine principal, so not flagged; also the verbatim Trio sentence describes a DIFFERENT lineup (Jormin/Falt) than plays on Parish, which is slightly misleading though literally true per wiki.
